+private final class AppControllerScreenContextPrefetcher: ScreenContextPrefetching, @unchecked Sendable {
+    var resolveResult: ScreenContextPrefetchResult?
+    private(set) var startCalls: [[String]] = []
+    private(set) var cancelCallCount = 0
+    private(set) var resolveCallCount = 0
+
+    func start(customWords: [String]) {
+        startCalls.append(customWords)
+    }
+
+    func cancel() {
+        cancelCallCount += 1
+    }
+
+    func resolve() async -> ScreenContextPrefetchResult? {
+        resolveCallCount += 1
+        return resolveResult
+    }
+}
+

+import CoreGraphics
+import XCTest
+@testable import GhostPepper
+
+final class ScreenContextTests: XCTestCase {
+    func testRecognizedScreenTextOrdersObservationsByReadingOrderAndAveragesConfidence() {
+        let observations = [
+            ScreenTextObservation(
+                text: "Bottom",
+                boundingBox: CGRect(x: 0.10, y: 0.10, width: 0.20, height: 0.05),
+                confidence: 0.40
+            ),
+            ScreenTextObservation(
+                text: "Top Right",
+                boundingBox: CGRect(x: 0.50, y: 0.80, width: 0.20, height: 0.05),
+                confidence: 0.60
+            ),
+            ScreenTextObservation(
+                text: "Top Left",
+                boundingBox: CGRect(x: 0.10, y: 0.79, width: 0.20, height: 0.05),
+                confidence: 0.80
+            )
+        ]
+
+        let result = RecognizedScreenText.fromObservations(observations)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(result?.text, "Top Left Top Right Bottom")
+        XCTAssertEqual(try! XCTUnwrap(result).averageConfidence, 0.60, accuracy: 0.0001)
+    }
+
+    func testFrontmostWindowSelectionReturnsFirstEligibleWindowForActiveApplication() {
+        let windows = [
+            FrontmostWindowCandidate(windowID: 10, ownerPID: 99, layer: 0, alpha: 1.0, frame: CGRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 20, height: 20)),
+            FrontmostWindowCandidate(windowID: 11, ownerPID: 42, layer: 3, alpha: 1.0, frame: CGRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 20, height: 20)),
+            FrontmostWindowCandidate(windowID: 12, ownerPID: 42, layer: 0, alpha: 0.0, frame: CGRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 20, height: 20)),
+            FrontmostWindowCandidate(windowID: 13, ownerPID: 42, layer: 0, alpha: 1.0, frame: CGRect(x: 5, y: 6, width: 30, height: 40)),
+            FrontmostWindowCandidate(windowID: 14, ownerPID: 42, layer: 0, alpha: 1.0, frame: CGRect(x: 10, y: 10, width: 40, height: 50))
+        ]
+
+        let result = FrontmostWindowSelection.selectFrontmostWindow(for: 42, windows: windows)
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(result?.windowID, 13)
+        XCTAssertEqual(result?.frame, CGRect(x: 5, y: 6, width: 30, height: 40))
+    }
+
+    func testScreenContextServiceReturnsNilAndLogsWhenScreenshotIsUnavailable() async {
+        let screenshotCapturer = ScreenContextScreenshotCapturer()
+        let textRecognizer = ScreenContextTextRecognizer()
+        let diagnostics = ScreenContextMessageRecorder()
+        let sensitiveMessages = ScreenContextMessageRecorder()
+        let service = ScreenContextService(
+            screenshotCapturer: screenshotCapturer,
+            textRecognizer: textRecognizer,
+            screenRecordingPermissionStateProvider: { .granted },
+            diagnosticLogger: { diagnostics.append($0) },
+            sensitiveLogger: { sensitiveMessages.append($0) }
+        )
+
+        let result = await service.captureContext(customWords: ["OpenAI"])
+
+        XCTAssertNil(result)
+        XCTAssertEqual(textRecognizer.recordedCustomWords, [])
+        XCTAssertEqual(diagnostics.messages, ["No frontmost window screenshot available."])
+        XCTAssertEqual(sensitiveMessages.messages, [])
+    }
+
+    func testScreenContextServiceLogsSuccessAndSensitiveTextSeparately() async {
+        let screenshotCapturer = ScreenContextScreenshotCapturer()
+        screenshotCapturer.result = makeImage()
+        let textRecognizer = ScreenContextTextRecognizer()
+        textRecognizer.result = RecognizedScreenText(
+            text: "Xcode Build Settings",
+            averageConfidence: 0.92
+        )
+        let diagnostics = ScreenContextMessageRecorder()
+        let sensitiveMessages = ScreenContextMessageRecorder()
+        let service = ScreenContextService(
+            screenshotCapturer: screenshotCapturer,
+            textRecognizer: textRecognizer,
+            screenRecordingPermissionStateProvider: { .granted },
+            diagnosticLogger: { diagnostics.append($0) },
+            sensitiveLogger: { sensitiveMessages.append($0) }
+        )
+
+        let result = await service.captureContext(customWords: ["Xcode"])
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(result, "Xcode Build Settings")
+        XCTAssertEqual(textRecognizer.recordedCustomWords, [["Xcode"]])
+        XCTAssertEqual(diagnostics.messages, ["Captured frontmost window text for cleanup context."])
+        XCTAssertEqual(sensitiveMessages.messages, ["Xcode Build Settings"])
+    }
+
+    func testScreenContextServiceAddsPermissionContextOnlyWhenRelevant() async {
+        let screenshotCapturer = ScreenContextScreenshotCapturer()
+        screenshotCapturer.error = NSError(
+            domain: "ScreenContextTests",
+            code: 7,
+            userInfo: [NSLocalizedDescriptionKey: "Capture failed"]
+        )
+        let textRecognizer = ScreenContextTextRecognizer()
+        let diagnostics = ScreenContextMessageRecorder()
+        let service = ScreenContextService(
+            screenshotCapturer: screenshotCapturer,
+            textRecognizer: textRecognizer,
+            screenRecordingPermissionStateProvider: { .denied },
+            diagnosticLogger: { diagnostics.append($0) },
+            sensitiveLogger: { _ in }
+        )
+
+        let result = await service.captureContext(customWords: [])
+
+        XCTAssertNil(result)
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            diagnostics.messages,
+            ["Failed to capture frontmost window text because screen recording permission appears unavailable: Capture failed"]
+        )
+    }
+
+    func testScreenContextPrefetchResolveReturnsCapturedContextAndClearsTask() async {
+        let dateProvider = SequencedDateProvider(
+            values: [
+                Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 10),
+                Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 10.42)
+            ]
+        )
+        let recordedWords = ScreenContextWordRecorder()
+        let prefetcher = ScreenContextPrefetcher(
+            captureContext: { words in
+                recordedWords.append(words)
+                return "Xcode"
+            },
+            dateProvider: dateProvider.currentDate
+        )
+
+        prefetcher.start(customWords: ["OpenAI"])
+        let result = await prefetcher.resolve()
+        let secondResolution = await prefetcher.resolve()
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(recordedWords.values, [["OpenAI"]])
+        XCTAssertEqual(result?.context, "Xcode")
+        XCTAssertEqual(result?.elapsed ?? 0, 0.42, accuracy: 0.0001)
+        XCTAssertNil(secondResolution)
+    }
+
+    func testScreenContextPrefetchCancelDropsPendingResult() async {
+        let prefetcher = ScreenContextPrefetcher(
+            captureContext: { _ in
+                try? await Task.sleep(for: .milliseconds(10))
+                return "Ignored"
+            }
+        )
+
+        prefetcher.start(customWords: [])
+        prefetcher.cancel()
+        let result = await prefetcher.resolve()
+
+        XCTAssertNil(result)
+    }
+
+    private func makeImage() -> CGImage {
+        let colorSpace = CGColorSpaceCreateDeviceRGB()
+        let context = CGContext(
+            data: nil,
+            width: 1,
+            height: 1,
+            bitsPerComponent: 8,
+            bytesPerRow: 4,
+            space: colorSpace,
+            bitmapInfo: CGImageAlphaInfo.premultipliedLast.rawValue
+        )!
+        context.setFillColor(CGColor(red: 1, green: 1, blue: 1, alpha: 1))
+        context.fill(CGRect(x: 0, y: 0, width: 1, height: 1))
+        return context.makeImage()!
+    }
+}
+
+private final class ScreenContextScreenshotCapturer: WindowScreenshotCapturing {
+    var result: CGImage?
+    var error: Error?
+
+    func captureImage() async throws -> CGImage? {
+        if let error {
+            throw error
+        }
+        return result
+    }
+}
+
+private final class ScreenContextTextRecognizer: WindowTextRecognizing {
+    var result: RecognizedScreenText?
+    var error: Error?
+    private(set) var recordedCustomWords: [[String]] = []
+
+    func recognizeText(in image: CGImage, customWords: [String]) async throws -> RecognizedScreenText? {
+        _ = image
+        recordedCustomWords.append(customWords)
+        if let error {
+            throw error
+        }
+        return result
+    }
+}
+
+private final class SequencedDateProvider {
+    private let lock = NSLock()
+    private var values: [Date]
+
+    init(values: [Date]) {
+        self.values = values
+    }
+
+    func currentDate() -> Date {
+        lock.withLock {
+            if values.isEmpty {
+                return Date(timeIntervalSince1970: 0)
+            }
+
+            return values.removeFirst()
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+extension SequencedDateProvider: @unchecked Sendable {
+}
+
+private final class ScreenContextMessageRecorder: @unchecked Sendable {
+    private let lock = NSLock()
+    private var storedMessages: [String] = []
+
+    var messages: [String] {
+        lock.withLock {
+            storedMessages
+        }
+    }
+
+    func append(_ message: String) {
+        lock.withLock {
+            storedMessages.append(message)
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+private final class ScreenContextWordRecorder: @unchecked Sendable {
+    private let lock = NSLock()
+    private var storedValues: [[String]] = []
+
+    var values: [[String]] {
+        lock.withLock {
+            storedValues
+        }
+    }
+
+    func append(_ value: [String]) {
+        lock.withLock {
+            storedValues.append(value)
+        }
+    }
+}

@@ -110,3 +110,13 @@
 - Moved cleanup prompt assembly, model resolution, deterministic corrections, and output sanitization into `Sources/CleanupShared`, then reused that support from both the app pipeline and the probe while fixing post-cleanup correction application in the app.
 - Implemented the probe CLI contract end to end: one-shot and interactive modes, inline or file-backed window context, stored prompt and correction preference loading with CLI precedence, read-only UserDefaults access, transcript rendering, and focused parser/runtime tests.
 **Summary:** Delivered the cleanup probe CLI as a real debugging surface over the shared local cleanup runtime. Ghost Pepper now ships a standalone probe that can build on demand, load either named or explicit cleanup models, mirror the app's stored cleanup preferences without mutating them, and exercise the same prompt, sanitization, and deterministic-correction behavior as the app itself. During wrap-up, the requirement index drift for STORY-0494 and STORY-0539 through STORY-0541 was corrected so those stories now trace to the iteration that actually delivered the probe rather than to an earlier backend-only pass.
+
+## ITER-0011
+
+**Completed:** 2026-04-10
+**Stories delivered:** STORY-0038, STORY-0047, STORY-0193, STORY-0260, STORY-0261, STORY-0262, STORY-0263, STORY-0264, STORY-0265, STORY-0266, STORY-0267, STORY-0268, STORY-0269, STORY-0270, STORY-0271, STORY-0272, STORY-0273, STORY-0274, STORY-0275, STORY-0276, STORY-0277, STORY-0278, STORY-0279, STORY-0280, STORY-0281, STORY-0282, STORY-0283, STORY-0284, STORY-0285, STORY-0286, STORY-0287, STORY-0288, STORY-0512, STORY-0532, STORY-0838
+**Tasks executed:**
+- Added the screen-context runtime: frontmost-window selection, single-window ScreenCaptureKit screenshots, Vision OCR with preferred-transcription custom vocabulary, reading-order text assembly, diagnostic and sensitive logging, and a cancellable prefetch lifecycle that returns OCR text plus elapsed time.
+- Wired OCR prefetch through `AppController` so eligible recording sessions launch screen capture before audio start, failed starts and termination cancel pending prefetch work, stop-and-transcribe resolves prefetched context into cleanup requests and archive entries, and post-paste learning stays enabled in settings while remaining inert without Screen Recording permission.
+- Expanded controller and screen-context coverage for permission-aware OCR diagnostics, prefetch lifecycle transitions, prompt-context delivery, performance-trace OCR timing, and the existing settings-shell recovery prompt and persistence contracts that this iteration now traces to completion.
+**Summary:** Delivered the OCR screen-context slice on top of the existing cleanup pipeline. Ghost Pepper now captures the frontmost eligible window with ScreenCaptureKit, recognizes window text with Apple Vision, starts OCR prefetch at recording start when cleanup and frontmost-window context are both available, resolves the prefetched context after recording stops, records OCR timing in the dictation trace, archives resolved window text with the session, and cancels pending OCR work during failed starts and application termination. During wrap-up, the scope audit confirmed that the Screen Recording recovery prompt, OCR and post-paste-learning settings toggles, and the persistence contracts for `frontmostWindowContextEnabled` and OCR prefetch disposal were already present and are now traced to this iteration rather than left pending.
